text.title: vui-accessibility-inclusivity Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for vui-accessibility-inclusivity as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: LLMs assuming English idioms and contractions are universally understood.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Non-native speakers process idioms with additional cognitive load, increasing misinterpretation. Agents must be prompted to lint and rewrite for ESL users with explicit reading-level constraints.

failure-mode.description: Relying solely on synthesized accent test data for WER evaluation.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Voice clones flatten real prosody. Synthetic data underestimates failure rates for real-world accented speech. Complement with Common Voice recordings or commissioned real-user samples.

failure-mode.description: Treating high ASR confidence scores as reliable for all demographic groups.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Confidence scores are not equally calibrated across groups. High-confidence misrecognitions happen more often for accented speech, creating a false sense of reliability for exactly the population most at risk.

failure-mode.description: Designing accent calibration UX that prompts users to "speak more clearly."
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Such prompts are othering and damage user trust. Accommodation must be invisible: backend model selection, extended timeout, and fallback paths — not instructions directed at the user.
